overview.short: Sheetla Mata Temple ( SHEET-lə MAH-tə, Hindi: [ʃɾiː ˈʃiːtlaː maːˈtaː ˈməndɪr]) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the goddess Shitala (Sheetla), wife of Guru Dronacharya who was the te
overview.description: Sheetla Mata Temple ( SHEET-lə MAH-tə, Hindi: [ʃɾiː ˈʃiːtlaː maːˈtaː ˈməndɪr]) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the goddess Shitala (Sheetla), wife of Guru Dronacharya who was the teacher of the Pandavas and Kauravas according to an Indian epic Mahabharata. The temple is located on Sheetla Mata Road in old Gurugram (earlier known as Gurgaon) city of Gurugram district in the state of Haryana in India. The shrine is visited by large numbers of people during Navaratri and other festivals.

topPlaces.name: Dronacharya Temple, Gurugram
topPlaces.description: The Dronacharya Temple is one of the only Hindu temples in India that is dedicated to Dronacharya, the teacher of the Pandavas and Kauravas in the Mahabharata. It is located in the Bhim Nagar village of Gurugram, Haryana, India.

topPlaces.name: MDLR Airlines
topPlaces.description: MDLR Airlines was an airline based in Gurgaon, Haryana, India. It operated scheduled domestic services.

topPlaces.name: 1958 Dan-Air Avro York crash
topPlaces.description: The 1958 Dan-Air Avro York crash was a fatal accident involving an Avro York cargo aircraft operated by Dan Air Services Limited on a non-scheduled international all-cargo service between Karachi, Pakistan, and New Delhi, India. The aircraft crashed on 25 May 1958 during an emergency landing at Gurgaon, Haryana, India, after an engine had caught fire en route from Karachi to Delhi.

topPlaces.name: Gurgaon
topPlaces.description: Gurgaon (Hindi: [ɡʊɽɡãːw]), officially named Gurugram ([ɡʊɾʊɡɾaːm]), is a satellite city of Delhi and administrative headquarters of Gurgaon district, located in the northern Indian state of Haryana. It is situated near the Delhi–Haryana border, about 30 kilometres (19 mi) southwest of the national capital New Delhi and 268 km (167 mi) south of Chandigarh, the state capital.

topPlaces.name: Sukhrali
topPlaces.description: Sukhrali is a census town in Gurgaon district in the Indian state of Haryana.

topPlaces.name: Gurgaon Rural
topPlaces.description: Gurgaon Rural is a census town in Gurgaon district in the Indian state of Haryana.

topPlaces.name: Carterpuri
topPlaces.description: Carterpuri (formerly Daulatpur Nasirabad) is a village in Gurgaon district of Haryana, India. Presently Carterpuri Nambardar is Manohar lal.

topPlaces.name: Chakkarpur
topPlaces.description: Chakkarpur is a large village located in Sector 28 of Gurugram, Haryana, India, along the Mehrauli–Gurugram Road. Known for its peaceful environment, the village is predominantly inhabited by members of the Yadav(Gotra Nuniwal) community.

items.note: The Dronacharya Temple is one of the only Hindu temples in India that is dedicated to Dronacharya, the teacher of the Pandavas and Kauravas in the Mahabharata. It is located in the Bhim Nagar village of Gurugram, Haryana, India.
MDLR Airlines was an airline based in Gurgaon, Haryana, India. It operated scheduled domestic services.

The 1958 Dan-Air Avro York crash was a fatal accident involving an Avro York cargo aircraft operated by Dan Air Services Limited on a non-scheduled international all-cargo service between Karachi, Pakistan, and New Delhi, India. The aircraft crashed on 25 May 1958 during an emergency landing at Gurgaon, Haryana, India, after an engine had caught fire en route from Karachi to Delhi.

Gurgaon (Hindi: [ɡʊɽɡãːw]), officially named Gurugram ([ɡʊɾʊɡɾaːm]), is a satellite city of Delhi and administrative headquarters of Gurgaon district, located in the northern Indian state of Haryana. It is situated near the Delhi–Haryana border, about 30 kilometres (19 mi) southwest of the national capital New Delhi and 268 km (167 mi) south of Chandigarh, the state capital.
Sukhrali is a census town in Gurgaon district in the Indian state of Haryana.
Gurgaon Rural is a census town in Gurgaon district in the Indian state of Haryana.
Carterpuri (formerly Daulatpur Nasirabad) is a village in Gurgaon district of Haryana, India. Presently Carterpuri Nambardar is Manohar lal.

Chakkarpur is a large village located in Sector 28 of Gurugram, Haryana, India, along the Mehrauli–Gurugram Road. Known for its peaceful environment, the village is predominantly inhabited by members of the Yadav(Gotra Nuniwal) community.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sheetla Mata Mandir Gurgaon?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sheetla Mata Mandir Gurgaon is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sheetla Mata Mandir Gurgaon?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Indira Gandhi International Airport, Delhi (11 km) and the nearest railway station is New Delhi Railway Station (26 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sheetla Mata Mandir Gurgaon?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sheetla Mata Mandir Gurgaon at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sheetla Mata Mandir Gurgaon?
faq.a: Stays in Sheetla Mata Mandir Gurgaon start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sheetla Mata Mandir Gurgaon?
faq.a: The top attractions are Dronacharya Temple, Gurugram, MDLR Airlines, 1958 Dan-Air Avro York crash, Gurgaon, Sukhrali.
